title: Lepanthopsis microlepanthes
intro: Lepanthopsis microlepanthes, commonly known as the Micro-lepanthes Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lepanthopsis microlepanthes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Lepanthopsis microlepanthes is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Lepanthopsis microlepanthes is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, one of the largest subfamilies within the orchid family. Within the subfamily Epidendroideae, Lepanthopsis microlepanthes is part of the tribe Lepantheae. Lepanthopsis microlepanthes is also classified as being in the genus Lepanthopsis within the tribe Lepantheae.
